description:
Buzz Lightyear is on a mission to rescue Woody from the evil Al McWhiggin!
Explore 15 levels, collect Pizza Planet Tokens, and defeat the Prospector
to save Woody and infinity and beyond!

1.0.0.description:
To infinity and beyond! **Toy Story 2: Buzz Lightyear to the Rescue** (PS1) is now playable in Archipelago! Help Buzz rescue Woody by exploring levels, collecting Pizza Planet Tokens, and finding everything Andy's toys need across the multiworld.

> Runs on **BizHawk** (2.10+) with the **Nymashock** PSX core. Targets the **NTSC-U** release (`SCUS-94464`). Ships with a custom **Toy Story 2 Client** and a full setup guide.

## Game Modes
- **Open Mode** — Every level is available from the start. Hunt down Level Unlocks to access them and play in any order you like.
- **Linear Mode** — Play the game in order. Each area unlocks after you defeat a boss, and you'll fight **every** boss along the way.

## Goals (Open Mode)
Choose what it takes to save Woody — any combination of:
- **Pizza Planet Tokens** (reach a token gate)
- **Defeated Bosses** (beat a set number of bosses)
- **Level Unlock** (unlock the Final Showdown)
- ...or any mix of the three!

In **Linear Mode**, each boss (Bombs Away!, Slime Time, Toy Barn Encounter, The Evil Emperor Zurg, and the Final Showdown) sits behind its own configurable Pizza Planet Token gate.

## Items
**Moves** (Buzz's abilities)
- Progressive Laser, Spin, Stomp, Double Jump, Pole Climb, Ledge Grab, Pole Vault, Push, Rope Sliding, Visor

**Gadgets** (per level)
- Cosmic Shield, Rocket Boots, Disc Launcher, Grappling Hook, Hover Boots

**Missing Toy Parts**
- Missing Ear, Missing Eye, Missing Arm, Missing Foot, Missing Mouth

**Level Unlocks**
- All 15 stages: Andy's House, Andy's Neighborhood, Bombs Away!, Construction Yard, Alleys and Gullies, Slime Time, Al's Toy Barn, Al's Space Land, Toy Barn Encounter, Elevator Hop, Al's Penthouse, The Evil Emperor Zurg, Airport Infiltration, Tarmac Trouble, and the Final Showdown

**Tokens & Tickets**
- Pizza Planet Token, Final Showdown Ticket

**Coin Bundles** (one per coin level, for Coinsanity)
- Andy's House, Andy's Neighborhood, Construction Yard, Alleys and Gullies, Al's Toy Barn, Al's Space Land, Elevator Hop, Al's Penthouse, Airport Infiltration, Tarmac Trouble

**Missing Toys** (per coin level)
- Sheep, Soldier, Worker Tike, Duck, Chick, Alien, Mouse, Critter, Passenger Tike, Luggage

## Sanities
- **Movesanity** — Shuffle everything Buzz can do! Includes **LITE (Weapons)** — only Laser, Spin, Stomp, Visor — and **LITE (Traversal)** — only Double Jump, Pole Climb, Ledge Grab, Pole Vault, Push, Rope Sliding.
- **Coinsanity** — Coins send checks based on how many you gather in a single run through a level. Your coins also come back to you as items. Make Hamm proud!
- **Lifesanity** — Every life is a check! Lives no longer grant a life and won't respawn, so play carefully.
- **Batterysanity** — Every battery is a check! They still heal you, but they won't respawn.
- **Green Laser Sanity** — Green Lasers are checks now, and they won't respawn either. Use them wisely!
- **Rexsanity** — Talking to Rex gives checks!
- **Hint Block Sanity** — Turns all hint blocks into checks.

## Traps
- **Cutscene Trap** — Hope you like Toy Story 2, because you're gonna watch it a *lot*.
- **Narrow Vision Trap** — I can't see! I can't see!
- **Damage Buzz Trap** — Ouch!
- **Freeze Buzz Trap** — Buzz must have run out of batteries!
- **Invincible Enemies Trap** — I swear I hit him!

Each trap's frequency is individually weighted (Off / Low / Medium / High), and you can set what percentage of filler gets replaced with traps.

## Filler
- **1 Life**
- **Extra Battery**

Both have configurable weights (Low / Medium / High).

## Quality of Life
- **Collect Enemy Coins Automatically** — Grabs enemy coins for you (great with Coinsanity).
- **Skip Cutscenes** — Marks all cutscenes as watched at the start (doesn't bypass the Cutscene Trap).
- **Disable Falling Animation** — Buzz always lands on his feet!
- **Auto Save** — Saves to slot 10 every time you hit the map. Recommended if you're worried about game overs.
- **Disc Launcher Pick Ups Fill Pockets** — Disc Launcher pickups fill you to max.
- **On-Screen Item Feed** — Shows item activity in the top-left of BizHawk (Off / Sent / Received / Both). Press **Select** in-game to cycle modes live.

## Music Randomizer
- **Normal** — Randomizes the game's music one-to-one.
- **Chaos** — You never know what you're going to get!
- **Oops All Bangers** — Pick one song and hear it for *every* level. (22 songs to choose from!)
- **Skip Song** — In a level, hold **Triangle** and press **L2 + R2** to change the song (disabled in Oops All Bangers — you dug your grave!).

## Death Link
- Optional **Death Link** support — if Buzz dies, everyone else with Death Link on goes down too, and vice versa. (Pairing with Auto Save is recommended!)

## Other Options
- **Skips** — Logic difficulty for tricks: **Off**, **Easy** (anyone can do them), or **Hard** (for the glitch hunters).
- **Pizza Planet Token Pool** — How many tokens are in the pool (30–99).
- **Starting Levels** (Open Mode) — Begin with 1–5 levels unlocked.
- **Omit Airport Infiltration / Elevator Hop** (Open Mode) — Keep these out of your starting levels if you don't have Stomp / Visor yet.
- Fully configurable **token gates** for the Final Showdown and every Linear Mode boss.

---

**Huge thanks to everyone who helped test and shape this release! But a special thanks to:**
- **Gooper**
- **MarioSpore**
- **Thedragon005**
- **Hooligan960**

Grab the latest `toystory2.apworld`, follow the setup guide, and go save Woody. A **PopTracker** pack is on the way!
1.0.0.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.0.0 — Initial Release

1.0.1.description:
# Changes

- Fixed Rocket Boots in Tarmac Trouble causing level to disintegrate
- Fixed wrong files being saved in apworld
- Set default option for On Screen Text to "Both"
1.0.1.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.0.1

1.0.2.description:
# Fixes

- Added Archipelago.json
- Properly Built Apworld
- Made it so if you make crazy settings, important items are given early
- Ran Apworld through several generation tests to confirm it's usability. (I would consider 98 out of 100 a win)

### PLEASE NOTE: While it is possible for you to make crazy settings, the ap will not gen if you make an impossible combination. The generation will warn you of this, so please set your settings responsible
1.0.2.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.0.2

1.0.3.description:
# Fixes

- Generation Issues with movesanity have been fixed (Please note that there is a 5% chance you have have to re-gen if you have movesanity selected. This is due to the Pizza Planet Tokens fighting over the moves for slots)

# Changes

- Logic changes for Andy's House and Construction Yard
1.0.3.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.0.3

1.1.0.description:
_June 2026_

This release adds the **all-in-one Lua script**, PopTracker support, a new filler
item, two new quality-of-life options, and a batch of logic and gameplay fixes.

> **⚠️ This release adds a new item to the pool, so existing seeds are not
> compatible.** You'll need to update the apworld and **regenerate** to use it.

---

## One script to rule them all — the all-in-one Lua

No more juggling multiple Lua scripts. A single **all-in-one (combined) Lua** now
handles everything — game connection, auto-tracking, traps, filler, QOL, music,
and death link — from one file you load in BizHawk. This is now the supported
script going forward.

**Please read the setup guide for how to load it** (the
`[README.md]`). If you're coming from an older setup, switch to the all-in-one script.

## New: PopTracker support

A full **PopTracker pack** is now available for Toy Story 2. It connects to your
Archipelago server and auto-tracks your progress — maps for every level, a Level
Select hub with a per-level Coinsanity overview, and a complete item panel.
Universal Tracker is also supported. See the pack's own release notes for setup.

## New: TS2 Client Tracker

The TS2 client now has Universal tracker baked in! No more need to open Universal Tracker!
Unless you just really want to!

## New filler

- **Invincible Buzz** — _"Can't touch this!"_ Grants Buzz **30 seconds of
  invincibility** while in a level. Added to the filler weighting options
  (default **Medium**). If a death link arrives while it's active, the death
  link takes priority.

## New quality-of-life options

- **Start Every Level With Full Health** _(default: on)_ — _"I feel like this
  should have been in the vanilla game!"_ Buzz starts every level at full health.
- **Never Game Over** _(default: on)_ — No longer forces the game to reset on a
  game over. Instead, if you're one hit away with no lives left, you're booted
  back to the map with 5 lives. Highly recommended for Coinsanity!

## Logic fixes & adjustments

- **Reviewed and corrected access logic across all 15 levels**, 
  with numerous individual fixes and adjustments so checks land in the
  right spheres for your settings.
- **Movement / gadget gating corrections**, including cases where a level's
  native gadget (e.g. Al's Toy Barn rocket boots) could be used without the item
  that's supposed to grant it.

## Fixes

- **Death link: Buzz can no longer move or take damage while dying.** He's now
  frozen and invulnerable for the duration of the death animation, then released
  cleanly on respawn — no more drifting or getting hit mid-death.
- **Your own item receipts now show in the client log.** A filter intended only
  to suppress the redundant in-game "sent" line was also hiding self-world item
  receipts from the log; in solo/own-world seeds this made the log look silent.
- **Reconnecting no longer re-delivers one-shot filler and traps.** Previously,
  resyncing after a disconnect could re-apply already-received lives, batteries,
  and traps (e.g. lives being refilled on every reconnect). The client now treats
  filler/traps already received before a reconnect as delivered.
  
## Known Issues

- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down

## Upgrade instructions

1. Replace your `toystory2.apworld` with the 1.1.0 build.
2. **Regenerate** your seed (the new filler item changes the pool — old seeds
   won't work).
3. Relaunch the Archipelago client.
4. Load the **all-in-one Lua** in BizHawk (see the setup guide).
1.1.0.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.1.0

1.1.1.description:
**REMINDER TO PLEASE SEE VERSION 1.1.0 FOR NEW LUA INSTRUCTIONS**


## Additions
- Added support for start_inventory_from_pool

## Fixes

- Fixed bug that caused end goal conditions to always require level unlock
- Easy Skips should now work with Hard Skip enabled
- Logic corrections in Andy's House Garage
- Added clarification on setup for the lua file. It must be in "Archipelago\data\lua" in order for it to work.
  
## Known Issues

- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down

## Upgrade instructions

1. Replace your `toystory2.apworld` with the 1.1.0 build.
2. **Regenerate** your seed (the new filler item changes the pool — old seeds
   won't work).
3. Relaunch the Archipelago client.
4. Load the **all-in-one Lua** in BizHawk (see the setup guide).
1.1.1.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.1.1

1.1.2.description:
# Updates

- Coinsanity coin-bundle items are now classified per level. The first bundles needed to reach 50 coins count as progression, the rest as useful.
- Pizza Planet Tokens are classified the same way. Progressive up until your maximum gate count. The rest will be classified as useful.

# Fixes

- Several and I mean SEVERAL logic bugs were patched. Notably, Al's Toy Barn was a mess
- Progressive Laser 3 will now display your disc launcher count

# Known Issues

- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down
- The Second Zurg Cutscene doesn't play from a trap. (I fought this one the entire development... I don't care anymore)
- There may be a bug that freezes your controls if you use Visor and get the Narrow Vision Trap. This is still unknown
1.1.2.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.1.2

1.1.3.description:
# Updates
- The game no longer takes you to the in-game Save Menu after exiting a level, only boss fights. This is to help keep players from accidently saving their games and soft locking themselves

# Bug Fixes
- Death Link no longer supersedes the "No Game Game Overs" QOL
- Losing all of your lives with the "No Game Game Overs" QOL enabled, no longer tricks the game into thinking you won the fight... you lost... try again

# Logic Fixes
- Around 24 logic issues and new skips have been corrected/added in the following levels:
  - Andy's House
  - Andy's Neighborhood
  - Al's Toy Barn
  - Elevator Hop
  - Airport Infiltration
- Airport Infiltration had several logic issues involving Missing Toys and Hamm. These have been corrected

# Known Issues
- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down
- The Second Zurg Cutscene doesn't play from a trap. (I fought this one the entire development... I don't care anymore)
- There may be a bug that freezes your controls if you use Visor and get the Narrow Vision Trap. This is still unknown
1.1.3.title: Toy Story 2 AP 1.1.3

2.0.0.description:
The big one. 2.0.0 turns every coin in the game into a check, rebuilds the
entire logic system from a single master sheet, adds new progressive weapons
and new traps, and brings the PopTracker pack fully in line. This covers
everything since the Coinsanity planning started.

> **Heads up:** 2.0.0 is not backwards-compatible with 1.0.x seeds. Location
> IDs, item IDs, and the datapackage changed. Re-roll fresh and update the
> PopTracker pack to 2.0.0 to match.

---

## Coinsanity (COMPLETE OVERHAUL)
- **Every coin in the game is now a check** — 822 coin locations across all ten
  levels.
- **`Coinsanity`** toggle to turn the whole system on/off.
- **`Coinsanity Checks Bundle Size`** — how many coins make up one check:
  `1 / 5 / 10 / 15 / 20 / 25 / all` (default **1**, i.e. every single coin is its
  own check. Bundles 5 and greater work like the old Coinsanity).
- **`Coinsanity Received Bundle Size`** — how many coins each received item grants:
  `1 / 5 / 10 / 15 / 20 / 25 / 50` (default **5**).
- **Per-coin detection & suppression** — in size-1 mode each collected coin fires
  exactly one check and is then suppressed so it can't re-fire.
- **Descriptive coin names** — in size-1 mode every coin reads as a real,
  named spot rather than a number.
- **Coin persistence** — collected coins now stay collected across deaths and
  reloads, disconnects, a-syncs and so on, tracked via AP datastorage.

## Full Logic Overhaul
- **Rebuilt from a single source of truth** — all location logic is now compiled
  from one master logic spreadsheet..
- **New rule compiler** — an expression-based compiler replaces all 177
  hand-written rules. `rules.py` went from ~2,350 lines to ~765, and a new
  `logic_data.py` holds the compiled rule data.
- **New "Insane" skip tier** — `Skips` is now `Off / Easy / Hard / Insane`.
  Insane layers the most demanding tricks on top of everything in Easy and Hard.
- **Dozens of logic corrections** from live audits, from practically every level!

## New & Reworked Items
- **Progressive Spin** — L1 base spin → L2 → **L3 "Super Spin"** (hold **O** to
  toggle it on/off).
- **Dizzy Buzz** *(trap)* — spins Buzz out into a dizzy stumble. *"I don't feel
  so good…"*
- **Determination to Save Woody** — Does nothing! Hooray! *(filler)*.
- **Missing Toy** handling — the five Missing Part items plus a
  **`Missing Toy Bundle Size`** option (`1 / 5`).

## New Options
- `Coinsanity` + the two coin bundle-size options (above).
- `Skips: Insane` tier.
- `Missing Toy Bundle Size`.
- `Determination Filler Weight`.
- `Local Filler` — keep your filler in your own world.
- **Filler weights can be set to `off`**, with a Determination-to-Save-Woody
  fallback so a seed never ends up with an empty filler pool.
- **`Dizzy Buzz Trap Weight`** (default **Low**), sitting in the Traps & Filler
  options section with the other trap weights.
  
## Removed Options
- `Auto Collect Coins From Defeated Enemies` — This was removed and switched
to always on. Turning this off would have made coinsanity not work, so as a fail safe,
it will always be on.

## Goal & Locked-Gate Messages
- New **on-screen flashing message** when you reach a gate or boss you can't
  enter yet — it tells you exactly what you're still missing (tokens / bosses).
- Cleaner goal-condition wording, including correct open-mode labels.

## Bug Fixes
- **Free-location bug** — a parenthesis-vs-dash name mismatch had been making 59
  non-coin locations always-reachable. Fixed.
- Tarmac Trouble at-start coin not being detected — fixed.
- Final Showdown not unlocking under the tokens goal — fixed.
- Coin-bundle auto-release bug — fixed.
- Several locked-gate-message bugs (instant flash zero-out, RAM relocation,
  name/message buffer sharing, boss-gate name tail).

### HUGE THANKS TO Phlimbob84 & Thedragon005 for Coin Mapping! And Gooper for figuring out I could do it in the first place

## Known Issues
- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down
- The Second Zurg Cutscene doesn't play from a trap. (I fought this one the entire development... I don't care anymore)
- There may be a bug that freezes your controls if you use Visor and get the Narrow Vision Trap. This is still unknown
- Coinsanity will sometimes cause enemies to blink into existence briefly! Spooky...
2.0.0.title: Toy Story 2 AP 2.0.0

2.0.1.description:
# Logic
- Corrections in the following levels:
  - Andy's House
  - Andy's Neighborhood
  - Al's Toy Barn
  - Airport Infiltration

# Bugs
- You can now no longer activate Spin Lv3 while in the air. This should allow you to Stomp properly now.

# Known Issues
- Double Jump will sometime works if Buzz is jumping into angled geometry. It's not easy to replicate and probably will never be fixed
- Saving the game could potentially freeze the game. There's no need to save since you have the qol save state and the AP will give you everything on a load. Also keep in mind that the PS1 version of this game takes forever to load in general. Give it enough time to think
- Cutscene warping may still be a thing. Do the right thing and leave a level if you're not supposed to be there... but also let me know, this bug is hard to pin down
- The Second Zurg Cutscene doesn't play from a trap. (I fought this one the entire development... I don't care anymore)
- There may be a bug that freezes your controls if you use Visor and get the Narrow Vision Trap. This is still unknown
- Coinsanity will sometimes cause enemies to blink into existence briefly! Spooky...
2.0.1.title: Toy Story 2 AP 2.0.1
